title: L3+ IA finalisation; URL mapping (Current vs. New URL mapping (Current pages > New IA pages). outlining consolidated sections and pages that will be removed in the new Information Architecture.; Category landing pages (5+) with enhanced copy; Product landing pages (5+) with enhanced copy
description: L3+ IA finalisation; URL mapping (Current vs. New URL mapping (Current pages > New IA pages). outlining consolidated sections and pages that will be removed in the new Information Architecture.; Category landing pages (5+) with enhanced copy; Product landing pages (5+) with enhanced copy for Public Web / OMCOZA / Personal / Retirement. Scope items: L3+ IA finalisation | URL mapping (Current vs. New URL mapping (Current pages > New IA pages). outlining consolidated sections and pages that will be removed in the new Information Architecture. | Category landing pages (5+) with enhanced copy | Product landing pages (5+) with enhanced copy. Planned delivery details: start 2026-06-04; end 2026-06-08; effort 3 days. Upstream dependency: We need stakeholder sessions in diaries already locked in for the the programme duration. Sign-off process defined. Current status: In Progress. Source comment/context: Dependencies * Stakeholder socialisation and buy-in: Must be completed prior to engagement commencing. * Active stakeholder participation is required throughout: As extended team members and thinking partners, not passive reviewers. * A clearly defined sign-off process: Must be in place before work begins. Risks * SEO standards not finalised: SEO governance is still being developed. Content decisions may need to be revisited as standards are finalised, making this a living document process rather than a single sign-off. * Tenant hierarchy misalignment: Some clusters (e.g. OM Insure) have not yet aligned to sharing a platform / real estate with internal tenant brands (e.g. iWyze). This has the potential to create structural conflict in our section and category templates and may require additional stakeholder resolution time. * Scope creep: Timelines are based on an agreed list of sites (OMCOZA, OMCOM, Bula Tsela, and LTD) and agreed scope. Any additional sites or out-of-scope requests will require a formal timeline and resource extension. Web and My Old Mutual (Secure): Unfamiliar territory - Additional timeshas been allocated to them, however, we have a number of unknowns in those two streams.
upstreamDeliverable: We need stakeholder sessions in diaries already locked in for the the programme duration. Sign-off process defined.
comments: Dependencies * Stakeholder socialisation and buy-in: Must be completed prior to engagement commencing. * Active stakeholder participation is required throughout: As extended team members and thinking partners, not passive reviewers. * A clearly defined sign-off process: Must be in place before work begins. Risks * SEO standards not finalised: SEO governance is still being developed. Content decisions may need to be revisited as standards are finalised, making this a living document process rather than a single sign-off. * Tenant hierarchy misalignment: Some clusters (e.g. OM Insure) have not yet aligned to sharing a platform / real estate with internal tenant brands (e.g. iWyze). This has the potential to create structural conflict in our section and category templates and may require additional stakeholder resolution time. * Scope creep: Timelines are based on an agreed list of sites (OMCOZA, OMCOM, Bula Tsela, and LTD) and agreed scope. Any additional sites or out-of-scope requests will require a formal timeline and resource extension. Web and My Old Mutual (Secure): Unfamiliar territory - Additional timeshas been allocated to them, however, we have a number of unknowns in those two streams.
